C-PRESET-ORCHESTRATION-PROFILES.IntentSummary: Add a shared orchestration profile domain, extend canonical schema v3 and presets without breaking old documents, resolve one startup policy for vv-controller prompt plugins, neutralize the managed base prompt, expose scoped CLI and status diagnostics, document the behavior, and synchronize durable graph and verification projections.

Baseline.Note: Canonical vvoc schema v3 has no orchestration section, built-in presets only control model roles, and missing configuration resolves through existing global, project, environment, and default layers.
SystemContextInjectionPlugin and WorkflowPlugin currently inject broad guidance that is not selected by a preset-configured concrete work policy.
The current graph and verification projections do not contain M-ORCHESTRATION-PROFILES, M-CLI-ORCHESTRATION, or matching verification anchors.

Target.Note: One internal resolver owns stable profile names, backward-compatible balanced fallback, strict parsing, capabilities, and exact concrete policy strings without depending on VvocConfig.

Prompt-producing plugins resolve the selected profile once from the startup snapshot and inject only compatible vv-controller guidance without changing tools, permissions, workflow state, or persistence.
Public CLI, schema, presets, status, completions, README, graph, and verification artifacts remain synchronized.

Warnings.Warning: The active C-CODEX-PRESET-LIMITS bundle overlaps src/lib/vvoc-config.ts, preset behavior and tests, completion tests, README.md, .grace/graph/main.xml, and .grace/verification/main.xml. Reconcile or archive that approved change before executing this plan so baseline and observed write scopes are not stale.

T-001.Title: Create the orchestration profile domain model and focused tests.

AcceptanceCriteria.Criterion: Stable names are exactly single-session, balanced, and orchestrated in that order.
Missing orchestration resolves to balanced, while blank and unknown explicit values fail with all allowed choices.
Each resolved controller policy contains only active-profile instructions; single-session permits only the documented reviewer exception, balanced is selective, and orchestrated is tracked.
MODULE_CONTRACT, MODULE_MAP, CHANGE_SUMMARY, and focused contracts are present and truthful.

T-002.Title: Extend strict schema v3 and canonical configuration with backward-compatible orchestration fields.

AcceptanceCriteria.Criterion: Root and preset orchestration.profile accept only supported values and remain optional in schema v3.
Old valid v3 documents resolve to balanced, fresh rendering writes balanced, and built-in presets render declared profiles.
Invalid root and preset values report precise JSON paths; sync preserves the selected root and custom preset fields while refreshing managed preset profiles.

T-003.Title: Apply preset role and orchestration changes atomically.

AcceptanceCriteria.Criterion: Every built-in preset has the approved profile mapping.
Applying a preset validates all roles and profile data before one vvoc.json write and never rewrites OpenCode config.
Custom presets without orchestration preserve the current profile, profile-only changes still write, invalid data leaves original bytes unchanged, and output reports actions and restart guidance.

T-004.Title: Inject one concrete vv-controller system context from the startup snapshot.

AcceptanceCriteria.Criterion: vv-controller receives universal guidance plus exactly one selected controller policy; build and custom primaries receive universal guidance only.
single-session excludes proactive working-subagent and tracked-loop instructions while retaining the concise no-working-subagent rule and reviewer exception.
Explore guidance and known-subagent exclusion remain intact, repeated hooks do not duplicate contexts, and post-construction config changes do not change policy.

T-005.Title: Select profile-compatible WorkflowPlugin guidance without changing enforcement.

AcceptanceCriteria.Criterion: single-session receives concise tracked review-only mechanics, balanced receives optional bounded delegation guidance, and orchestrated retains the full tracked protocol.
Review-only failures remain findings and never route to implementers.
Tool registration, access control, result parsing, repair, persistence, transitions, hard stops, and round limits remain behaviorally unchanged and restricted to vv-controller.

T-006.Title: Make the managed vv-controller prompt profile-neutral.

AcceptanceCriteria.Criterion: The template preserves universal findings-first, working-state, assumption, reroute, editing, skill, large-feature gate, hard-stop, artifact, and reporting guidance.

The template defers concrete work behavior to the selected system policy and does not prescribe ordinary explore, investigator, implementer, change_with_review, implementation work-item, or reviewer-loop routes.
vv-review stays findings-only, vv-execute stays specialized, and managed prompt loading remains valid.

T-007.Title: Add managed skill visibility regression coverage.

AcceptanceCriteria.Criterion: vv-execute discovery metadata hides inline, classic, subagent-driven, and current-session alternatives.
The loaded vv-execute body still requires explicit inline or classic choice before mutation.
The loaded vv-review body still opens review_only work items, launches only reviewers, treats FAIL as findings, and stops before fixes.

T-008.Title: Add scoped orchestration show and set commands.

AcceptanceCriteria.Criterion: show defaults to effective scope, reports profile and source, resolves missing orchestration to balanced, and never writes.
set accepts only supported values and global or project scope, preserves unrelated config, rejects invalid existing files without byte changes, and bootstraps canonical v3 when absent.
Unchanged explicit profiles are kept without rewrite and changed output reports path and restart guidance.

T-009.Title: Register the orchestration command and shell completions.

AcceptanceCriteria.Criterion: vvoc orchestration is a top-level command.
Bash, zsh, and fish suggest show and set, then all three profile values after orchestration set.
Top-level completion expectations remain synchronized with src/cli.ts.

T-010.Title: Report the resolved orchestration profile in installation status.

AcceptanceCriteria.Criterion: Global, project, and default status reports print the profile from the selected source.
No-file effective status reports balanced and default source.
Invalid explicit profiles remain parse problems without mutation, and all existing diagnostic output remains intact.
README documents commands, profile values, built-in mapping, partial custom preset behavior, source display, and restart requirements exactly as implemented.

README states that profiles are prompt-driven and do not disable tools, distinguishes reviewer subagents from delegated working context, preserves vv-review findings-only behavior, and explains vv-execute explicit choice.

T-012.Title: Synchronize GRACE module ownership and orchestration data flows.

AcceptanceCriteria.Criterion: M-ORCHESTRATION-PROFILES and M-CLI-ORCHESTRATION appear in index ownership, routed direct anchors, module anchors, and modules.
Every changed source and template file has accurate ownership and dependencies include M-ORCHESTRATION-PROFILES where required.
Preset switch, system context, workflow guidance, and new orchestration profile data flows describe current behavior truthfully.

T-013.Title: Synchronize GRACE verification and run final release-grade gates.

AcceptanceCriteria.Criterion: New verification anchors appear in index ownership, routed direct anchors, verification anchors, and module verification.
Critical flow verification covers backward compatibility, atomic writes, source resolution, prompt isolation, and fail-closed explicit values.
All targeted tests, GRACE lint and status, release consistency, repository check, build, and package checks pass with fresh output.
The final diff remains within DurableScope and ObservedWriteScope after overlap reconciliation.

Verification.Command: bun test src/lib/orchestration.test.ts src/lib/opencode.test.ts src/lib/managed-agents.test.ts src/lib/managed-skills.test.ts src/commands/config-validate.test.ts src/commands/preset.test.ts src/commands/orchestration.test.ts src/commands/completion.test.ts src/commands/status.test.ts src/plugins/system-context-injection.test.ts src/plugins/workflow.test.ts
